title: Environment Variables from ConfigMap
description:
## Environment Variables from ConfigMap

ConfigMaps can inject configuration as environment variables into pods, keeping application images generic and portable.

**Your task:**

1. Create a ConfigMap named `app-env` with two keys:
   - `LOG_LEVEL=debug`
   - `APP_PORT=8080`

2. Create a Pod named `env-pod` using `busybox:1.36` with command `sleep 3600` that loads **all keys** from `app-env` as environment variables using `envFrom`.

**Verify:**
```bash
kubectl exec env-pod -- env | grep -E 'LOG_LEVEL|APP_PORT'
```

hints.title: Create the ConfigMap
hints.body: Use `kubectl create configmap` with multiple `--from-literal` flags.

hints.title: Load all ConfigMap keys via envFrom
hints.body: Use `envFrom` with `configMapRef` to load all keys at once — simpler than mapping each key individually.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: env-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep","3600"]
    envFrom:
    - configMapRef:
        name: app-env
EOF
